interviews.title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: A Tribute to Reverend John Garcia Bensel, The Pastor of the Jazz Community

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: I can think of any. I mean I see some of the old timers that are still around like Joe Wilder and Jackie Williams, of course Milt Hinton just passed away but he was part of it. I played with him back in the 50s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: I even got to play with Roy Haynes playing drums... I mean Jimmy Campbell who had been with Woody earlier on, Jackie Williams, Earl Palmer. Anybody who would come in would want to sit in and so I’d play bass and somebody else would play piano

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: We have a new record coming out in the next couple of weeks on Chiaroscuro that we recorded on the Norway, with Keter Betts on Bass and Jackie Williams on drums, Junior and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: We did the session at night, myself and Earl May and Jackie Williams. And Earl and Jackie played a tune with me before. That’s usually the trio I use most of the time now
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Al Vollmer
about_quotes.evidence: I picked up Zeke on the east side uptown, Jackie Williams on the west side downtown... I’ve taken the Harlem Blues and Jazz Band like to various parts in the world
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Vollmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, June 8, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Slam Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Marshall on drums, you know, and of course, I mentioned before, Johnny Guarnieri on piano, Jimmy Shirley on guitar and Jackie Williams on drums.
